reasons: AC1-4 covered by live Redux state mutations plus DOM assertions against the mounted PerpsDepositToast component.
AC6 covered by live absence assertion plus affected Jest selector coverage; trace shows all ac<N>- nodes passed.
AC5 and AC7 are explicitly UNTESTABLE in-browser with concrete rationale, not silently skipped.
better_version_guidance: A stronger farm slot would provide a safe mocked Perps order/deposit backend so AC5 can be exercised without placing a live order.
Expose the toast selector through a stable eval_ref to prove AC6 entirely through runtime selector output instead of combining recipe and Jest evidence.

graph_integrity.reason: Workflow graph dry-ran successfully; forbidden-pattern scan found no bad prefixes, manual nodes, long waits, or switch defaults.

ac_coverage.reason: Every AC is either PROVEN or explicitly UNTESTABLE with rationale; zero WEAK or MISSING rows remain.

evidence_contract_basics.reason: Visual toast claims have screenshots; internal ownership claims have trace-backed state and DOM assertions.

runtime_observability.reason: Live recipe log has no HUD warnings; summary reports 19/19 passed and no console/runtime recipe issues.

suggested_recipe_delta: Add a reusable eval_ref for selectEvmTransactionsForToast once the recipe runtime can import UI selectors safely.
Add a safe mock order submission fixture for Perps so order-toast suppression can be tested end-to-end.

good_patterns: Save and restore original Redux state around synthetic transaction-state assertions.
Use ac<N>-prefixed node IDs and screenshot filenames for direct coverage mapping.
